page: Amazon Web Services m6in.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores running at 3.5 GHz. The system includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, particularly in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests, all scoring in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also strong, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 4976.73 GB/sec. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves strong results in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, though it exhibits weak performance in text generation for large 70B parameter language models.

The m6in.24xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for network-heavy workloads due to its high baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring users to provision external EBS volumes, which may affect overall storage latency and cost. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high network throughput make this instance appropriate for database operations, static web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores operating at 3.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong throughput for static web serving and Redis operations, while single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation remain average to weak. This hardware profile is well-suited for network-intensive applications, database operations, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, optimized for network-heavy enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.24xlarge features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A network-optimized general-purpose instance featuring ninety-six Intel vCPUs and balanced memory configuration for enterprise workloads.

384.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput networking
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU hardware, requiring external EBS volumes
Appropriate for database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded workloads
